title: Control Totals Validation
text:
0.1 What it shows
This dashboard summarizes and validates the output of the control_totals pipeline. Each chapter is a self-contained Jupyter notebook in summary_scripts/.
Each chapter compares one or more forecasts side-by-side using Quarto tabsets — one tab per forecast configured in config.yaml.
The first chapter (Region) shows region-wide totals. The remaining six chapters break each indicator down by target area (the target_id geography defined in control_target_xwalk), with one section per PSRC county (King, Pierce, Snohomish, Kitsap):
Year columns are picked per forecast (each forecast may have a different base year, targets end year, and final end year). County “Total” rows are recomputed from the underlying counts; ratio “Total” rows are recomputed from the summed numerator and denominator (not averaged).

title: Control Totals Validation
text:
Population — rebased_control_totals_pop
Household Population — rebased_control_totals_hhpop
Households — rebased_control_totals_hh
Housing Units — rebased_control_totals_units
Household Size — household population ÷ households
Vacancy Rate — 1 − (households ÷ housing units)

title: Control Totals Validation
text:
0.2 Forecasts in this dashboard

Not scaled

Legacy LUVit (RTP26)
2021 targets
Not scaled
Legacy Control-Totals spreadsheet (RTP26, 2025-06-11)



Both Vision forecasts and Summer 2026 use the updated 2026 county targets; Legacy LUVit (RTP26) carries the older 2021 targets. Vision 2060 Scaled has been rescaled to match the REMI v3.3 regional forecast, and Vision 2060 Scaled by County has been rescaled to match REMI v3.3 independently per county — the others retain their native target totals.

title: Control Totals Validation
text:
0.3 Pointing the dashboard at runs
The file config.yaml lists the forecasts to display. Each entry is either a pipeline forecast (a directory with data/pipeline.h5 produced by control_totals/run.py) or a spreadsheet forecast (a legacy LUVit-style Control Totals Excel workbook).
forecasts:
  # Pipeline run — years come from configs/settings.yaml
  - name: Vision 2060 Scaled
    path: ../../examples/vision_2060_scaled
    type: pipeline

  - name: Vision 2060
    path: ../../examples/vision_2060
    type: pipeline

  - name: Summer 2026
    path: ../../examples/summer_2026
    type: pipeline

  # Legacy Control Totals workbook — years are declared inline,
  # and an external xwalk maps target_id → county.
  - name: Legacy LUVit (RTP26)
    path: ../../examples/legacy_luvit_spreadsheet
    type: spreadsheet
    spreadsheet_file: output/Control-Totals-RTP26-2025-06-11.xlsx
    xwalk_path: ../../examples/vision_2060_scaled/data/control_target_xwalk.csv
    base_year: 2020
    targets_end_year: 2044
    end_year: 2050
Paths are relative to the validation/ folder. Edit config.yaml, re-execute the notebooks, then re-render the book.
Forecasts that do not contain data for a given indicator (for example, the legacy workbook does not include housing units, and a pipeline run that has not yet produced rebased control totals) render a small “Not available for this forecast” placeholder in their tab instead of breaking the page.

title: Control Totals Validation
text:
0.4 Rendering the book
Install Quarto and from the control_totals/validation/ folder run:
quarto render
Output is written to validation-dashboard/. Notebooks are not executed on render (execute.enabled: false); they ship with their committed outputs. To re-execute everything before rendering:
jupyter nbconvert --to notebook --execute summary_scripts/*.ipynb --inplace
quarto render
To render just one notebook (using its committed output):
quarto render summary_scripts/population.ipynb

title: Control Totals Validation
text:
0.5 Adding a new chapter

Drop a new .ipynb in summary_scripts/.
In any cell, import the shared helpers:
from validation_data_input import (
    show_indicator_county, show_ratio_county,
    show_region_indicator, show_region_ratio,
    target_area_table, ratio_table,
    format_counts, format_ratio,
    forecast_ids, forecast_name,
    COUNTIES,
)
The show_* helpers emit a Quarto panel-tabset with one tab per configured forecast. For ad-hoc analyses, use target_area_table, ratio_table, and the formatters directly with a specific forecast id (see forecast_ids()).
Execute the notebook (so its outputs are committed).
Add the path to the chapters: list in _quarto.yml.
quarto render.

title: Control Totals Validation
text:
0.6 Layout reference
validation/
├── _quarto.yml              book config + sidebar
├── index.qmd                this page
├── config.yaml              which forecast(s) to validate
├── styling/custom.scss      light/dark theme tweaks
├── PSRClogo2016path.png     PSRC logo (sidebar)
└── summary_scripts/
    ├── util.py              plotly + pandas helpers
    ├── validation_data_input.py   multi-forecast loaders + tabset helpers
    └── *.ipynb              one chapter per notebook

title: 9  Households comparison - 2050
text:
9.6 Notes

% Diff is computed as (A - B) / B * 100, where B (baseline) is the second forecast listed at the top of the notebook.
Cell color: red when |% Diff| >= 5%, amber when >= 2.5%, green otherwise.
Target areas present in only one forecast still appear, with - for the missing forecast’s value.
